videos.title: How to Spend 2 Days in HO CHI MINH CITY Vietnam | The Perfect Travel Itinerary
videos.description:
Prepare to immerse yourself in the vibrant energy of Ho Chi Minh City! Join us as we unveil the ultimate guide on how to make the most of your two unforgettable days in this captivating Vietnamese metropolis. From exploring stunning sights to savoring exciting experiences, this travel itinerary covers it all.

Our journey kicks off with a detailed day-by-day plan, highlighting the top attractions and activities in Ho Chi Minh City. We provide exclusive insider tips to help you fully embrace the city's unique charm, including hidden gems that go beyond the typical tourist spots.

Food enthusiasts, brace yourselves for a culinary adventure through the diverse cuisine of Ho Chi Minh City. We'll lead you through the city's gastronomic landscape, from the finest eateries to bustling markets teeming with fresh ingredients, and introduce you to mouthwatering local delicacies. Our recommendations span a wide range of dining experiences, from upscale restaurants to cozy cafes and lively street food stalls, ensuring you get a comprehensive taste of Ho Chi Minh City's flavors.

Additionally, we'll share practical advice on how to navigate Ho Chi Minh City, including the best times to visit popular attractions to avoid crowds and tips to make your stay enjoyable and hassle-free.

Whether it's your first time in Ho Chi Minh City or you're a seasoned traveler, our guide is packed with all the information you need for an unforgettable two-day adventure in this bustling Vietnamese city.

💲*To streamline your travel planning, we've converted all local currency prices into USD. This simple step means easy budgeting for our international travelers, ensuring a smooth and enjoyable trip without the fuss of currency calculations!*

hoian City and hinoi but heavy rains and flooding forc us to cancel those plans I did my advance research regarding the best neighborhood to stay in hoian city and I kept seeing District 1 but we time to do any sort of deep planning before arriving in a place so sometimes we learn the hard way we made two big mistakes and two good decisions let's start with mistake number one and that was not taking a guided tour of hman City the first few days that we got there it would have helped us to understand the city so much more taking a tour is something we normally do when we first get to a city anything in this first couple weeks actually originally all we wanted to do was decompress for a bit before jumping into the deep end we always recommend that you get a tour as soon as you can when you arrive D in a new city but unfortunately in hoochi Min City most of the tours are on the back of a motorbike not that we were 100% opposed to this but we had some reservations actually seeing all but this was such a great tour that we think whoever you are you should take it just be sure to wear good walking shoes it's a walking tour unfortunately if you have mobility issues this is not your tour but otherwise turn on your pedometer and let's start pumping those arms not really it's not a fast walk but you'll cover a lot of ground there are a few places where you'll travel with your guide by taxi or in our case a Vietnamese electric car service so your feet do get a few breaks this tour was fabulous it helped us to understand more about the city in every way food culture history it had everything you don't need to book this tour through Airbnb they have their own website and YouTube channel that you might find interesting and we've linked both ways in the description below had we taken this tour sooner we might have stayed in hoian city for a full month but as it was we took a loss on the last week at our Airbnb in order to spend time in Hanoi and perhaps redeem The Experience so a few facts about hoochi Min City it's the largest city in Vietnam with a population of over 8 million people it also has split up into 24 districts our guide taught us about a few key districts and in our opinion these are the the primary ones to consider unless you're living here full-time as an expat he said district one is for fun hotels bars night life District 3 Is For Living but also a great spot for tourists to stay District Four to be robbed this is ancient history but still was funny because it used to be a mafia driven place and District 5 is for food those giant towns there so let's talk briefly about each of the districts to give you an overview and then we'll talk to you about mistake number two District 1 has many of the luxury hotel and alongside there are high-end shops the Opera House government offices foreign embassies and several other larger buildings but it's also considered the tourist and Backpacker section of the city so it's got a very diverse group of people here many many online neighborhood recommendations will suggest this as the number one area to stay in the city especially for its busy nightlife scene but there's also a few problems with this District which we'll get into when we talk about our second mistake District 3 is a very residental IAL area with more green space than District 1 visiting here gives a better perspective on how the Vietnamese people live wandering through the neighborhoods showed us a more peaceful side to hoochi Min City it's known for its delicious local Cuisine Street carts are everywhere but they are crowded which is one indicator that the food is safe so you'll have a more authentic experience here it's a popular alternative to District 1 for tourists timeout magazine rated it 18th on a list of the world's 50 coolest neighborhoods we wish we had known about this sooner in District 4 what used to be a mafia run area has since been cleaned up by the government and is flourishing since the 1990s a lot of business have set up Offices here and District 4 has a cool Coffee Culture you can walk down a street and easily have a dozen or more coffee shops to choose from it's got a nice local vibe to it now and it has a huge Fresh Food Market that's primarily for locals with authentic foods that is always busy which is a good sign the drawback is that it's farther away from many of the tourist sites you want to visit so you need to take a grab to get around District 5 is the area known as Chinatown this is where you'll find most of the chinese vietnamese people Chinese Traders settled here during the 18th century and brought their Customs culture and traditions it's home to numerous temples and landmarks that reflect its Chinese influence you'll find Chinese medicine here with its herbs and potions and Eastern style Healthcare treatments you'll also find plenty of authentic restaurants before we talk about mistake number two we're excited to share that we've started our own absolutely free community Forum that we are calling La Familia you can ask questions about trip planning and all things related to travel there a video with the details is linked in the description below so what is mistake number two well first not knowing that District 1 has many different areas with some being much better for visitors we've been told that District 1 is the place to be if you're a tourist because it's Central to all the main sites but there's a busy multi-lane highway that separates the North and South portions of the district the north side has more options for museums activities and dining so our big mistake was we picked an Airbnb on the South Side what are some of the problems with the southide well first of all our area was very congested with motorbikes and air pollution also we didn't know how much effort it would take to navigate to get to the north side of the district and a lot of the food sold by the sidewalk Street fenders isn't always safe for tourists so let's let talk about motorbikes traffic and air quality because we lived in Cairo for a couple weeks we felt comfortable battling crazy traffic when crossing the streets but a lot of motorbike drivers view red lights or crosswalks as mere suggestions even when pedestrians are crossing and there are so many motorbikes in hingan city that they have to park them on the sidewalks and the worst part was our Airbnb building's first floor was a parking garage for hundreds of motorbikes so every time we had to go in and out or wait for an elevator to the ninth floor of our building where we were staying we were breathing in gas fumes and exhaust and about navigating to the north side because of all this traffic trying to cross a busy highway to get to the north side of the benan market was more effort than we wanted when headed to some place casual like a restaurant you put your life in your hands just a little bit when you are trying to cross these streets and it doesn't really matter that you have a green light we kept hearing about benan Market but we didn't pick up on it until too late that it's a very touristy market and the food stalls aren't that great and also some of them close early so we're finally here at the benan market so even though the market is a central point in the district we will give it a hard pass the souvenirs there are also way overpriced not that we ever buy souvenirs no space or interest getting back and forth from the south side of the district is not good for a nice casual walk after dinner to help your food de adust food is such a big part of how we adjust to a new city if we're angry or disappointed with our food it's seriously impacts our enjoyment of a place I'm not a fan of eating fish with their head still on or staring eyeball to eyeball with a fresh shrimp so some local seafood wasn't appealing but otherwise we really do love to eat Local Foods let see what you got here so this is a clay pot pork and then just plain rice and then a soup that goes with it I will need to look up exactly what it is but is that the sour soup or oh yeah it is it's it's sour soup every day food stalls would pop up right outside our building but we saw meat sitting out for hours with very little activity of people buying cooking or eating the food in those places and that's a huge red flag to us even our guide cautioned us against sampling certain street food he said he had people Miss early morning tours because of food issues I think we would have experienced more of Vietnamese cuisine if we were on the north side of the highway so our second good decision was deciding to detour and spend our final week in Hanoi we learned from M stakes in hoian city and booked a hotel in a busy Central area of Hanoi thanks to recommendations from others we stayed in the old quarter near Juan Kim Lake you have to weave around some motorbikes but there are fewer of them and the Lakeside promade gives you an easier path to walk than on the streets with broken sidewalks even though we didn't have time time to take a tour we had a great experience that felt like a lighter lift than our previous 3 weeks and we had an easier time finding local foods that felt safe to eat our hotel even offered a free breakfast which allowed us to try Vietnamese style breakfast foods we' found some great coffee shops and restaurants tucked away in side alleys our location checked off all the boxes for us to have a great visit so let's talk about what we paid our district one hoian city Airbnb cost $675 19 for a month which breaks down to only $22.51 per night our Oldtown Hanoi Hotel was $538 or $71.98 per night our three coolest neighborhoods tour was $742 for both of us so let's talk about what we would recommend and would we visit again we highly recommend booking a tour to get a lay of the land do it early our guide recommended that District 3 was the best and we agreed with that you'll get fresh local foods that you'll │ Top 10 things To Do In Ho Chi Minh City | 2024 Travel Guide

The top 10 attractions in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam, showcasing its vibrant culture and history. Highlights include Tao Dan Park, a serene green space, the intricately decorated Jade Emperor Pagoda, and the panoramic views from Bitexco Tower. The Saigon Opera House offers a glimpse into Vietnam's colonial past, while the War Remnants Museum presents a poignant perspective on the Vietnam War. Nguyen Hue Walking Street is a lively hub of local life and entertainment, and a boat trip on the Mekong River reveals the rural charm of Vietnam. The Notre Dame Cathedral and Ben Thanh Market are must-visit landmarks for their architectural and cultural significance. The Cu Chi Tunnels provide a deep dive into Vietnam's wartime history.

videos.transcript: hello and welcome to open Tiara today we're looking at the top 10 things to do in hoochi Min City formerly known as Saigon this bustling Metropolis in Vietnam is a mesmerizing blend of Rich history and culture stay tuned till the end for a complete and immersive experience of this city 10 toan Park this large public park is an oasis of of green in the middle of the city in the mornings you'll see locals practicing Tai Chi and doing exercise here there are wooded areas ponds streams flowers and ornamental plants making it a great spot for bird watching W the pathways relax on a bench or stop for a drink at the small Cafe you'll feel far away from the city traffic and Noise nine the Jade Emperor pagoda the Jade Emperor Pagoda is one of the most spectacular temples in hoochi Min City it was built in 1909 and honors the Supreme toist deity the Jade Emperor the temple is filled with intricate statues and carvings of both Buddhist and taist figures clouds of incense fill the air as locals come to pray and make offerings don't miss the turtle pond off to one side filled with turtles that symbolize longevity the elaborate roof tiles and colorful tiered Towers make this Temple truly gorgeous both inside and out8 by texco Tower this 68 story skyscraper dominates the city's Skyline and offers unbeatable 360° views from its sky deck on the 49th floor you can see many of hoochi min's landmarks like notredam Cathedral The Saigon River and more don't miss watching the sunet from here seeing the city lights flickering on as the sun dips below the Horizon is spectacular and if you're feeling adventurous head one story higher to the Eon 51 Restaurant and Lounge where you can enjoy drinks and food with an open air view seven Saigon Opera House The Saigon opera house is recognizable by its Bell Epoch architecture and arched window frame lining its white facade built in 1897 when Saigon was the capital of French Indochina it dazzles both inside and out like stepping back in time the red and gold hued interior is full of intricate carvings sophisticated chandeliers plush velvet seats and base murals depicting cherubs and Muses catching a ballet or musical theater performance here makes you feel part of of Vietnam's exclusive High Society from a century ago six the war remnants Museum the war remnants Museum documents the Vietnam War from the Vietnamese perspective through striking photography and artifacts some of the tanks planes and equipment on display outside grab your attention first inside you'll find emotive exhibits focused on the impact of US military tactics like agent orange and phosphorus bombs it's a sobering and intense experience but a must sea to understand the history and cost of the war on this country five guen way walking Street theen way street is located right in the heart of District 1 this pedestrian street bursting with local energy attracts both tourists and locals alike by day Marvel at the colorful Grand fountain in the middle of the long Central Plaza By Night Neon Lights Adorn flashy shopping centers and modern highrises on both sides stop at one of the busy cafes or kiosks selling everything from designer Brands to handmade crafts from local artists with street performances and pop-up concerts there's always entertainment four meong River explore Rural Life along the mighty meong River by boat as we Cruise past tiny Villages and jungle Greenery we'll stop to tour fruit orchards or sample freshly made coconut candy from a local family business make sure your camera is ready when we hit kyb's floating Market the boats overflow with tropical fruits vegetables and flowers as vendors paddle up to Shoppers boats to barter and sell sell their goods I can't think of a more quintessentially Vietnamese experience three notredam Cathedral the notredam cathedral was built during French colonial times in the late 1800s it's still an actively used Roman Catholic church today the Striking red brick facade features two tall towers and arched Windows lining the walls inside sunlight filter through vibrant stained glass windows as visitors quietly admire the Glorious interior sitting across from the bustling Central post office it's amazing such a peaceful Sanctuary can be found steps away from the chaotic City two benthan Market visit benan Market in the heart of hoochi mean cities District 1 this Maze of stores and shops sells everything from t-shirts and conical hats to Gourmet street food and fresh tropical fruit local Merchants display mounds of vietnames silks bamboo weaving Ceramics and laa WEA the air hangs thick with Aromas from sizzling meat patties faux noodle soup and sugar cane juice remember to bargain before buying handcrafted souvenir to take back home one coochi tunnels no visit to Southern Vietnam is complete without an immersive visit to the historic cooi tunnels a massive underground Network that played a vital role during the Vietnam War era located about 70 kilm Northwest of hoochi Min City this 125m long Maze of narrow tunnels was dug by Viet Kong soldiers and villagers in the 1940s at first to evade French colonists then later American troops descending below ground you almost feel transported back in time to experience what life was like for Viet Kong soldiers learn how clever camouflag trap doors kept the tunnels hidden despite heavy bombing see the simple beds where soldiers slept and the basic medical stations they used to treat the injured samples of the rice and tapioca root they survived on offer a bland but filling meal even try- aiming old rif and grenade launchers from wartime watchtowers to protect the entrance if you enjoyed this video on hoochi Min City you'll love this next one
